name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
window: Q2 FY26 · print 2026-07-16 (prior Q1 window Apr 16 → Jun 23 retained)

events.text: Q1 FY26: rev US$35.9B (+6.4% q/q, above the top of guide), GM 66.2% (+390bp q/q), OpM 58.1%, EPS NT$22.08, ROE 40.5%. HPC +20% q/q to 61% of revenue. FY26 rev guided 'above 30%' in USD (from 'close to 30%'); capex revised to the top of US$52-56B. N3 = 25% of wafer revenue, N5 36%, N7 13%.
Bernstein OP, PT $430 vs ~$351, calling TSM the 'most reliable compounder in AI' — 28% EPS CAGR through '28, +50% EPS this year, '27 capex $68B, trading at ~20% discount vs SOX.

Quantifying the server CPU opportunity: sizes the server-CPU market at ~$170bn by 2030 (units 23m→63m, 30% CAGR); server CPUs need ~200kwpm of leading-edge by 2030, of which TSMC serves 122kwpm — an opportunity of ~$44bn of TSMC revenue (11% of sales) in 2030, from $3.7bn/3% in 2025. Raises sales 3-7%/EPS 4-9% in 2027-30. Buy, PT NT$3,000.

CoWoS update: TSMC turning aggressive on CoWoS 2027, raising capacity to 115k/175k/220k WPM by end-26/27/28; total CoWoS consumption +86% this year / +30% next. N3 hot-run/super-hot-run pricing rising further in Q3, regular N3 hikes ~10% and N2 ~5-6% in Jan-27. EMIB vs CoWoS-L decision on Google TPU V9 expected late-2026/early-2027.

2027 semi outlook: supply-demand gap widens to 15-20% in 2027, N2/N3 util 110-120%, super-hot-run at a 15-20% premium; 2027 wafer-price hikes start at 10% including Apple (vs only +1-2% Apple in 2026) — lifting blended ASP ≥10% and GM into the 68-70% range. FY27 rev NT$7,057bn (+37%, Street-high). Raises TP to NT$3,500. Arizona P1 GM already ~50% after 2 years.

Asia Trip Takeaways: 3nm 'tight and nobody cutting anything'. 2026 hikes by customer: AI/HPC ~+10%, consumer ~+5%, Apple low-single-digit → blended ASP ex-2nm only +3-4%. 2027 +≥10% across ALL customers incl. Apple, with GM potentially reaching high-60s to 70%. Supply/demand gap widens to 15-20% in 2027. More positive tone on H2'26 GM (flattish vs guidance of a decline).

TSMC already at its prior peak of ~8M wafers/month with limited new capacity, so the agentic CPU surge could push the system into a broad wafer shortage; Samsung acknowledged the same risk. EUV constrains capex upside: 40-45 EUV units already booked for 2027 (memory makers competing for DRAM EUV), capping 2027 capex at ~$70-75B vs others' $80-85B.

Preliminary 2027 CoWoS allocation: raises 2027 CoWoS capacity assumption to 200kwpm by year-end (from 170k) = ~60% Y/Y AI-XPU growth. NVDA CoWoS-L ~910k (+40% Y/Y); Google TPU #2 via MediaTek 180k + Broadcom 365k; CPU becomes a material consumer of 2.5D — NVDA Vera 5.75mn / AMD Venice 6.75mn units in 2027 (vs ~1.25mn '26). Reiterates OW TSMC.

'Accelerating capacity build' into the 07-16 print: reiterate Buy, TP raised to NT$3,000. AI/HPC a multi-year structural growth engine with even stronger 2027 momentum over the past quarter — particularly AI accelerators + server CPUs — behind the accelerating capacity build-out.

'Expecting GM upside going forward' — reiterate Buy, PT NT$3,500 (top pick). 2Q26 to beat guide in USD & TWD; models 2Q GM ~67.1%. 2H26 GM confidence up on super-hot-run premium orders + faster Arizona ramp (P1 GM ~50% at fully-loaded UTR + 90% yield); thinks TSMC 'very likely' raises FY26 guide again to +30-35%. FY27 GM toward 70% (Fubon 68.8%), EPS NT$144.13 vs cons NT$127.84; FY27 capex US$75bn; N3 ~210k / N2 ~120k / CoWoS ~180k end-2027.

events.src: TSMC · 2Q26 earnings (release + call, C.C. Wei / Wendell Huang)
events.text: 2Q26 print: rev US$40.20bn (+12.0% q/q, +33.7% y/y USD) at the guide high end (record); GM 67.7% (above 65.5-67.5% guide), OpM 60.3%, EPS NT$27.25 (flattered by NT$63.2bn VIS disposal/MTM gains), net margin 55.6%, ROE 45.9%. 2nm 3% of wafer rev (first contribution), 3nm 30%, ≤7nm 77%; HPC 66%. 3Q26 guide rev US$44.6-45.8bn (mid $45.2bn), GM 65-67%, OpM 56-58% (N2 ramp + overseas dilute GM ~3-4pt). FY26 rev now guided 'slightly above 40%' in USD (from 'above 30%') — clears the most-watched bogey; but 3Q GM guide sits below the elevated buy-side bar → near-term debate is margin, not demand.

FY26 capital budget RAISED to US$60-64bn (from ~US$52-56bn Jan / ~US$56bn Apr), split ~70-80% advanced / ~10% specialty / ~10-20% advanced packaging-test-mask; ~US$10bn YTD raise attributed to demand + tool-price inflation. Next 3 years of capex 'even more significantly higher' than the prior 3 (no numeric 2027/28 guide). Additional US$100bn Arizona investment → US$265bn total planned ('four or more fabs, front and back end' for 2nm-and-below + advanced packaging). Clears the ~US$58bn buy-side bogey — the clearest positive read for the equipment/materials chain (ASML/semicap).

'Capex hike a strong indicator' — reiterate Buy, PT unchanged NT$3,500 (42% upside, 23x FY27F EPS). 2Q results/3Q guide 'generally in-line'; 2Q EPS NT$27.25 (10% above Fubon, 13% above cons) but market wanted 68-69% GM. RAISES FY26/FY27 EPS to NT$108.80 / NT$155.41 (from NT$103.90 / NT$144.13; +4.7%/+7.8%) vs cons NT$100.13 / NT$129.26 (market-high). Reads the capex raise as a leading indicator of 2027-28 growth; N3 to 210-220k / N2 to 120k by end-2027. Positive cross-read for INTC EMIB-T (CoWoS shortage) and MediaTek ASIC share.

cells: AI/HPC 'robust'; FY25 raised to ~30%; sovereign AI added
AI 'stronger than 3mo ago'; mid-40s CAGR 'a little better'
AI accel high-teens % of FY25 rev; CAGR raised to mid-/high-50s; FY26 ~30%
AI 'extremely robust'; agentic shift lifts tokens; FY26 raised 'above 30%'; CAGR toward higher-50s
HPC +20% q/q to 66% of revenue; AI demand 'extremely robust'; agentic AI adds CPU silicon demand; FY26 growth now 'slightly above 40%'
Maintained USD 38-42bn
Narrowed to USD 40-42bn
FY26 USD 52-56bn; next 3yrs '>>' prior USD 101bn
FY26 to high end (~USD 56bn); next 3yrs significantly higher
FY26 raised to USD 60-64bn; 70-80% advanced / ~10% specialty / 10-20% packaging-test-mask; next 3yrs 'even more significantly' higher
N2 on track 2H25; tape-outs > N3/N5; A14 in 2028
N2 HVM 'later this quarter'; faster ramp '26
N2 in HVM 4Q25, good yield; fast ramp '26; N2 dilutes GM 2-3pts
N2 ramping multi-phase; N3 capacity now expanded (new fabs); A14 2028
N2 = 3% of Q2 wafer revenue; steep ramp a 3-4pt H2 GM drag; A14 on track (+10-15% speed / -25-30% power), volume 2028; A13/A12 2029
'Narrowing the gap'; building back-end capacity
Front+back-end 'very tight'; 2 AZ AP fabs + OSAT
Back-end ~8% of FY25 rev, >10% in '26; faster than corporate
CoWoS 'very tight'; largest reticle; CoPoS pilot line, MP couple yrs out
Packaging 'so tight' it is limited by customer growth; welcomes alternatives (Intel EMIB-T) to close the gap; majority stays CoWoS; alt line ~1yr from maturity
GM 58.6%; FX -180bp; reaffirm 53%+ 'and higher'
GM 59.5%; overseas dilution cut to 1-2% FY25
GM 62.3% (FY 59.9%); LT raised to 56%+, ROE high-20s
GM 66.2%; N3 crosses corporate avg 2H26; pricing 'strategic, not opportunistic'
Q2 GM 67.7%; Q3 mid 66%; N2 + overseas dilute, offset by leading-edge demand/productivity; pricing value-based/partner-oriented, not opportunistic
6 AZ fabs; speeding Fab2 (N3) several qtrs
Speeding up; upgrading to more advanced nodes; near 2nd land
Fab2 HVM pulled to 2H27; Fab4 permits; 2nd land bought
2nd land secured; more strategic; cost structure improving
Additional US$100bn Arizona investment (→US$265bn total) for several N2-and-below logic fabs + advanced packaging; overseas GM dilution 2-3% early → 3-4% later
